title: Secrets of the Lost Tomb is back! 10th Anniversary Edition - Thoughts on the new campaign
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZcdQLCEyjjU

A short, topical update from October 24, 2023: *Secrets of the Lost Tomb* returns to crowdfunding today with a 10th Anniversary Edition Kickstarter, and Daniel (@dungeondive) — owner of the original plus every expansion bar the *menis*, and self-confessed *Lost Tomb*-is-#1 evangelist — explains why he's not backing it.

The headline reason: the new edition is **100% incompatible** with the original. No upgrade pack is possible; the campaign is the whole game in roughly thirteen boxes at $350–$400 all in. Daniel already owns the perfect game (perfect *for him*) and doesn't need a perfect upgrade. He's also out of hobby-room space — games are already stacking on the floor.

What's visible of the new version: cleaner, modern art and graphic-design (good — Daniel asked for exactly that in the masterclass), much nicer tile art (also good), but the new tiles **don't interlock** like the old puzzle-shape tiles do — a quiet loss for table re-arrangement. The early signal is that the core game loop is preserved, which is encouraging given how heavily Everything Epic re-engineered *Lost Station* and lost what made the original work.

Daniel closes with a Kickstarter-business-model gripe: the *all-in-up-front-or-never* structure forces non-Discerning consumption, and he hopes some expansions stay in print long enough for buyers to dip in incrementally.

----

Daniel's not backing the upgrade because he's content with the original. Has loving an original ever made you skip a 2.0 you would otherwise have grabbed in a heartbeat?
